entries.title: Evaporation
entries.definition: Liquid water turns into water vapour when heated by the sun.

entries.title: Transpiration
entries.definition: Plants release water vapour into the air through pores in their leaves.

entries.title: Condensation
entries.definition: Cooling water vapour turns back into tiny droplets that form clouds.

entries.title: Precipitation
entries.definition: Cloud droplets grow heavy and fall as rain, snow or hail.

entries.title: Infiltration
entries.definition: Water soaks down into the soil and rock, becoming groundwater.

entries.title: Surface Runoff
entries.definition: Water that flows over the land into streams and rivers.

entries.title: The Water Cycle
entries.definition: The continuous journey of water: evaporation, condensation, precipitation and runoff.

entries.title: Uneven Distribution
entries.definition: Water is spread unevenly, so some places face shortages while others have plenty.

entries.title: Water Contamination
entries.definition: Pollutants make water unsafe for people and ecosystems.

entries.title: Flood Causes
entries.definition: Heavy rain on sealed surfaces overwhelms drains, causing floods.

entries.title: Drought
entries.definition: A long dry period with little rainfall that causes water shortages.

entries.title: Water Management
entries.definition: Using, storing and recycling water wisely to make supplies last.

entries.title: Four National Taps
entries.definition: Singapore's water sources: catchment, imported water, NEWater and desalinated seawater.

entries.title: Forest Structure
entries.definition: Rainforest layers from top to bottom: emergent, canopy, understorey, forest floor.

entries.title: The Canopy
entries.definition: The dense roof of treetops that catches most of the sunlight.

entries.title: Equatorial Climate
entries.definition: Hot and wet all year round near the Equator.

entries.title: Drip Tips
entries.definition: Pointed leaf tips that let heavy rain run off quickly.

entries.title: Buttress Roots
entries.definition: Wide roots that support tall trees in the thin rainforest soil.

entries.title: Plant Adaptations
entries.definition: Drip tips shed rain, buttress roots give support, lianas climb for light.

entries.title: Biodiversity
entries.definition: The huge variety of plant and animal life found in an ecosystem.

entries.title: Deforestation
entries.definition: Clearing forests for farming, logging and building.

entries.title: Deforestation Effects
entries.definition: Loss of biodiversity, soil erosion, habitat destruction and more CO2.

entries.title: Conservation
entries.definition: Using natural resources sustainably so they survive for the future.

entries.title: Mangrove Environment
entries.definition: Warm, salty, muddy coasts flooded by the tides.

entries.title: Prop Roots
entries.definition: Stilt roots that anchor the tree in soft mud against tides and waves.

entries.title: Pneumatophores
entries.definition: Breathing roots that grow upward out of the mud to take in air.

entries.title: Value of Mangroves
entries.definition: Coast protection, wildlife nurseries and carbon storage.

entries.title: Coastal Protection
entries.definition: Dense mangrove roots absorb wave energy and reduce erosion.

entries.title: Mangrove Threats
entries.definition: Coastal development and aquaculture clear and destroy mangroves.
